pi_items.source: Code analysis: seed_products.php creates only SONY-WH1000XM5 and LOGI-MXM3S. Products APPLE-MBP16 and DELL-XPS15 do not exist in seed (seed has APPLE-MBA-M2-13 and DELL-U2723QE instead). setup_task.sh silently skips non-existent products when creating duplicates.

seed_products.php line 48 creates: ['sku' => 'BOSE-QCUE']. This is the actual SKU in the database. Metadata claims BOSE-QCU (missing trailing E), which is searched for in setup_task.sh line 31 and export_result.sh line 19, but won't match the actual database record.
setup_task.sh line 35: $variation->setPrice(new \Drupal\commerce_price\Price('329.00', 'USD')). The setup script corrupts the Bose price to exactly this value.

seed_products.php line 48 sets initial Bose price to $299.00. The metadata claims the 'correct' price is $429.00, which is neither the original price ($299.00) nor a documented restoration value. This appears to be an arbitrary target price.

seed_products.php creates exactly 12 products (lines 44-57). Only 2 of 4 claimed duplicate SKUs have source products, so only 2 duplicates can be created. Final: 12 + 2 duplicates - 2 deleted = 12 products remaining. Expected count of 10 is impossible with the actual seed data.

privileged_info_summary: The task setup has critical inconsistencies. The seed script creates 12 initial products, but only 2 of the 4 claimed duplicate source products exist (APPLE-MBP16 and DELL-XPS15 are missing; the seed has different SKUs). The Bose product SKU is BOSE-QCUE in the actual database but metadata claims BOSE-QCU, causing a mismatch. Only 2 of 4 duplicates can be created. The expected final count of 10 is incorrect; it should be 12. The target correct price of $429.00 is unverified and differs from the original seed price of $299.00.
